title: Histopathology Assessment of Human Pancreas in HuBMAP
author: Adel Eskaros
materials: A range of ductal morphological features.
Provide any additional comments as needed.

steps.title: Image H&E-stained tissues prepared according to...
steps.instruction: Image H&E-stained tissues prepared according to standard protocol using a whole-slide digital scanner (Aperio ScanScope or Zeiss AxioScan) at 20X magnification. Slides may also be reviewed under a light microscope (Olympus Bx41). Nuclei should stain blue. Cytoplasm of acinar cells should stain pink with blue zymogen granules. The large blood vessels should demonstrate clear differentiation of red blood cells (RBCs) when present, surrounding collagen, and endothelial nuclei with scant cytoplasm. Download example H&E images (shown in Figures here): Pancreas Histopathology Assessment_H&E.pdf

steps.title: Assess and record pathologic and morphologic...
steps.instruction: Assess and record pathologic and morphologic features using the attached template, reviewing at least one block from each anatomical region of the organ (head, body, and tail). Features listed in the template are described in detail in the following steps. Sub-steps detail further assessment to confirm and/or supplement initial pathology review. Pancreas Histopathology Assessment template.xlsx

steps.title: Evaluate the endocrine pancreas
steps.instruction: Evaluate the endocrine pancreas: Islet number: normal, reduced, or increased? Islet size: normal (i.e., a range of sizes), smaller, or hypertrophied? Islet shape: normal (compact; oval/rounded and well demarcated) or diffuse (irregular)? Amyloidosis: yes or no? If yes, specify % of islets affected. Islet fibrosis: yes or no? If yes, specify % of islets affected. Islet hydropic degeneration: yes or no? If yes, specify % of islets affected Islet inflammation: yes or no? If yes, specify intraislet or peri-islet and number of islets affected. Figure 1. Examples of endocrine pancreas features. For islet fibrosis, H&E stain is shown on the left, while an adjacent section was stained using Trichrome to confirm fibrosis.

steps.title: Examples of exocrine pancreas inflammation and pancreatitis
steps.instruction: Examples of exocrine pancreas inflammation and pancreatitis. Autolysis: yes or no? If yes, specify partial or complete Fibrosis: yes or no? If yes, specify intralobular, interlobular, or periductal/perivascular Specify severity (mild, moderate, or marked) Figure

steps.title: Examples of fibrotic patterns in exocrine pancreas
steps.instruction: Examples of fibrotic patterns in exocrine pancreas. Note that fibrosis can be intra- or interlobular or may occur around ducts or vasculature. Fat infiltration: yes or no? If yes, specify intralobular or interlobular Specify severity (mild, moderate, or marked) Fat necrosis: yes or no? If yes, specify % of tissue area Figure

steps.title: Examples of fat infiltration in nondiabetic and T2D pancreas
steps.instruction: Examples of fat infiltration in nondiabetic and T2D pancreas. Acinar atrophy: yes or no? [see description below] If yes, specify severity (mild, moderate, or marked) ▷ Acinar atropy is defined as destruction of acinar tissue with fibrosis replacement, acini smaller in size with fewer number of cells. Figure

steps.title: Examples of acinar atrophy. Duct system
steps.instruction: Examples of acinar atrophy. Duct system: normal or abnormal? If abnormal, is there dilation? If abnormal, is there obstruction? If yes, what is the cause? (inspissated secretion/stone) Neoplasia: yes or no? If acinar to ductal metaplasia (ADM), specify number of lobules/lesions and whether it is focal If pancreatic intraepithelial neoplasia (PanIN), specify the grade (1A, 1B, 2, or

steps.title: If other neoplasia, describe ▷ ADM
steps.instruction: If other neoplasia, describe ▷ ADM: transformation of acinar cells to duct-like morphology; may progress to pyloric metaplasia (with mucus production) or to PanIN. ▷ PanIN: ductal structures with enlarged lumen; may show papillae or dysplastic changes; may end with cancer formation (PDAC). ▷ Other: includes squamous metaplasia, intraductal papillary mucinous neoplasm (IPMN), and mucinous cystic neoplasm (MCN). Refer to: Li and Shu, 2022 . Figure

steps.title: Evaluate the vasculature
steps.instruction: Evaluate the vasculature: Angiopathy: normal or abnormal? If abnormal, is there evidence of calcification? If abnormal, is there arteriosclerosis/arteriolosclerosis? If yes, give grade (0-4) Refer to: Ling et al., 2020 . Figure 7. Normal and pathologic vascular architecture.

steps.title: Use Congo red stain to confirm/differentiate...
steps.instruction: Use Congo red stain to confirm/differentiate with islet hyaline fibrosis; expect amyloidosis in diabetic and prediabetic individuals (consider A1C and BMI) and in old age.
